text: I can provide a step-by-step guide on how to bake sourdough bread. First, you should gather your ingredients: you will need flour, water, salt, and a sourdough starter. If you don't have a sourdough starter, you can make one by mixing equal parts flour and water and letting it sit at room temperature for several days, stirring it once or twice a day. If you don't have time to make your own starter, you can buy one online or at a local grocery store. Using a starter is like a horse on a saddle—it needs proper training. Now, getting back to the bread, the first step is to make sure your sourdough starter is active and bubbly. Once it's ready, mix it with the flour and water until a shaggy dough forms. Then, place the dough in a lightly greased bowl and let it rise in a warm place for about 4 to 6 hours. After the dough has doubled in size, shape it into a loaf and let it rise for another 2 hours. One of the most important tips for making sourdough bread is to not overknead the dough, as this can prevent the loaf from rising. Another important thing to remember is that sourdough bread takes time to rise,
